description: Replace carb-target-first normal-meal allocation with a calories-first model that budgets the six canonical meals from daily energy, assigns per-meal carb strategies, derives meal carbs and fat from remaining calories after protein, and preserves existing training fueling behavior.

userStories.title: Compute the six-meal calorie pool
userStories.description: As a user, I want the six normal meals to be budgeted from day energy and training fueling so meal planning follows the new calories-first model.
acceptanceCriteria: Calculate the six normal-meal calorie pool as TDEE + training_calorie_demand_kcal - training_calorie_supply_kcal.
Keep training_calorie_supply_kcal equal to training_carbs_g * 4.
Existing TDEE, training calorie demand, and training fueling calculations remain unchanged.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Apply canonical calorie and protein meal shares
userStories.description: As a user, I want normal-meal calories and protein distributed predictably across the six canonical meals so the plan stays easy to follow.
acceptanceCriteria: Distribute the six normal-meal calorie pool across breakfast, morning-snack, lunch, afternoon-snack, dinner, and evening-snack using shares 2/9, 1/9, 2/9, 1/9, 2/9, 1/9.
Keep the daily protein target at 2 * weight_kg.
Distribute daily protein across the same six canonical meals using the same 2/9, 1/9, 2/9, 1/9, 2/9, 1/9 shares.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Assign baseline meal carb strategies
userStories.description: As a user, I want each canonical meal to have an explicit carb strategy derived from the selected carbs mode so meal composition is inspectable and deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: For --carbs normal, assign carbs_strategy=medium to all six canonical meals.
For --carbs low, assign carbs_strategy=low to all six canonical meals.
For --carbs periodized, assign baseline carbs_strategy=low to all six canonical meals before overrides.
Each canonical normal meal row includes carbs_strategy with one of low, medium, or high.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Apply periodized post-training overrides
userStories.description: As a user on periodized carbs, I want the correct post-training meals upgraded to high-carb status so the plan matches the specified timing rules.
acceptanceCriteria: For --carbs periodized, mark the meal at --training-before as the first post-training high-carb meal.
Mark the next chronological meal as the second high-carb meal except where explicit dinner and evening-snack exceptions apply.
Do not wrap from evening-snack to breakfast when assigning a second high-carb meal.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Apply dinner and next-day periodization exceptions
userStories.description: As a user, I want dinner and evening-snack edge cases handled exactly so periodized outputs are stable in late-day training scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: If --training-before=dinner, only dinner is forced high and evening-snack remains low unless another rule overrides it.
If --training-before=evening-snack, only evening-snack is forced high and no wrap-to-breakfast high meal occurs.
If --training-tomorrow=high, dinner is always high in periodized mode, including when this overrides the dinner or evening-snack exception outcomes.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Derive meal carbs and fat from remaining calories
userStories.description: As a user, I want each normal meal's carbs and fat derived from remaining calories after protein so the meal plan follows the calories-first model.
acceptanceCriteria: For each canonical normal meal, assign protein calories first from the meal protein grams.
Compute remaining meal calories after subtracting protein calories from the meal calorie budget.
For low strategy, convert 1/4 of remaining meal calories to carbs and 3/4 to fat.
For medium strategy, convert 2/3 of remaining meal calories to carbs and 1/3 to fat.
For high strategy, convert 3/4 of remaining meal calories to carbs and 1/4 to fat.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Recompute top-level carb and fat totals from meals
userStories.description: As a user, I want top-level carbs and fat to reflect the final emitted meal allocations so day totals match the new allocation logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Derive top-level carbs_g as the sum of final emitted meal carbs under the new allocation model.
Derive top-level fat_g as the sum of final emitted meal fat under the new allocation model.
Training meal carbs_strategy is always high.
Keep current training meal behavior and ordering unchanged apart from the new carbs_strategy field.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Handle protein-overflow meals with warnings
userStories.description: As a user, I want the planner to stay valid when a meal cannot fit its assigned protein so failures degrade predictably and visibly.
acceptanceCriteria: If assigned protein calories exceed a meal's calorie budget, reduce that meal's protein allocation until remaining calories are 0 or greater.
If protein reduction occurs, top-level protein_g equals the sum of final emitted meal protein.
Do not derive carbs or fat from negative remaining meal calories.
Emit protein-reduction warnings to stderr only on otherwise successful runs.
Do not add warning fields to JSON, text, or table payloads.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add regression coverage for calorie shares and totals
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want automated coverage for the new calorie and macro allocation rules so core calculations do not silently regress.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover the six-meal calorie split and protein split using shares 2/9, 1/9, 2/9, 1/9, 2/9, 1/9.
Tests cover top-level carbs_g and fat_g being derived bottom-up from final meal allocations.
Tests cover normal, low, and baseline periodized carbs_strategy assignment.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add regression coverage for periodized and warning edge cases
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want focused tests for late-day periodization and protein-reduction handling so edge-case behavior remains deterministic.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover periodized edge cases for --training-before=dinner and --training-before=evening-snack.
Tests cover --training-tomorrow=high forcing dinner to high in periodized mode.
Tests cover protein-reduction warning emission and non-negative remaining-calorie handling.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update canonical docs for calories-first meal allocation
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want canonical docs updated so the new allocation model and output semantics are documented and stable.
acceptanceCriteria: Update docs/REQUIREMENTS.md with the calories-first meal allocation rules and periodized override behavior.
Update docs/MODEL.md with carbs_strategy output and top-level carb and fat totals being derived from final meal allocations.
Update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and docs/PLAN.md with the new engine responsibilities and canonical enhancement plan.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
